mainEntity.name: How do I optimize SVG files for web?

acceptedAnswer.text: Upload your SVG to our optimizer above, or use SVGO in your build pipeline. Key steps: remove editor metadata, simplify path coordinates, merge redundant groups, and minify the output. This typically reduces file size by 30-70%.

mainEntity.name: Is it safe to optimize SVG files? Will it change how they look?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, SVG optimization is safe. It removes invisible data (metadata, comments, hidden elements) and simplifies coordinates. The visual output is identical — only the file size changes. Always preview the optimized result before deploying.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between SVG minification and optimization?

acceptedAnswer.text: Minification removes whitespace and formatting. Optimization goes further: it restructures paths, merges elements, removes unnecessary attributes, and converts shapes to simpler forms. Our tool does both.
